Sub-problem 1:  
The number of rooms requiring attention follows a Poisson distribution with a mean of 20. The probability mass function for a Poisson distribution is given by:

$$ P(X = k) = \frac{e^{-\lambda} \lambda^{k}}{k!} $$

where $ \lambda = 20 $ and $ k = 15 $. Substituting these values:

$$ P(X = 15) = \frac{e^{-20} \cdot 20^{15}}{15!} $$

Using a calculator or computational tool to evaluate this expression gives:

$$ P(X = 15) \approx 0.0486 $$

Thus the probability that exactly 15 rooms need attention on that day is approximately 0.0486.

Sub-problem 2:  
The concierge assists with 8 concerns and has a success rate of 90%. This is a binomial distribution with parameters $ n = 8 $ and $ p = 0.90 $. The probability of at least 7 successes is:

$$ P(X \geq 7) = P(X = 7) + P(X = 8) $$

Using the binomial probability formula:

$$ P(X = k) = \binom{n}{k} p^{k} (1 - p)^{n - k} $$

First calculate $ P(X = 7) $:

$$ \binom{8}{7} (0.90)^{7} (0.10)^{1} = 8 \cdot (0.90)^{7} \cdot (0.10) \approx 8 \cdot 0.4783 \cdot 0.10 \approx 0.3826 $$

Then calculate $ P(X = 8) $:

$$ \binom{8}{8} (0.90)^{8} (0.10)^{0} = 1 \cdot (0.90)^{8} \cdot 1 \approx 0.4305 $$

Adding these probabilities:

$$ P(X \geq 7) \approx 0.3826 + 0.4305 = 0.8131 $$

Thus the probability that at least 7 of the concerns are resolved successfully is approximately
